# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re
SEARCHBAR_TEMPLATE_PATH = '/workspace/netbox/utilities/templates/search/searchbar.html'

def test_searchbar_populates_value_from_query_param__a1():
    """
    The search bar should render the current query in the input's `value` attribute
    using proper escaping. It should not rely on autofocus/onfocus JS to populate it.
    """
    source = open(SEARCHBAR_TEMPLATE_PATH, encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'value="{{ request.GET.q|escape }}"' in source, 'Search input must render the query directly via value="{{ request.GET.q|escape }}" so it is populated on page load.'

def test_searchbar_populates_value_from_query_param__a2():
    """
    The search bar should render the current query in the input's `value` attribute
    using proper escaping. It should not rely on autofocus/onfocus JS to populate it.
    """
    source = open(SEARCHBAR_TEMPLATE_PATH, encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'value=""' not in source, 'Search input must not have an empty value="" attribute; it should be populated from request.GET.q.'

def test_searchbar_populates_value_from_query_param__a3():
    """
    The search bar should render the current query in the input's `value` attribute
    using proper escaping. It should not rely on autofocus/onfocus JS to populate it.
    """
    source = open(SEARCHBAR_TEMPLATE_PATH, encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'onfocus=' not in source, 'Search input must not rely on an onfocus handler to set the value; it should be rendered directly in the template.'
